items.title: Frank 'convinced' of Spurs stay amid Poch chants
items.summary: Tottenham head coach Thomas Frank said he is "convinced" he will remain in charge for the north London derby against Arsenal despite Spurs fans calling for him to be sacked.

items.title: Late Sesko goal rescues United point at West Ham
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko scored in the sixth minute of stoppage time to rescue a point for Manchester United, whose winning run under Michael Carrick came to an end with a 1-1 draw at West Ham.

items.title: Sources: De Zerbi to exit Marseille after PSG rout
items.summary: Roberto De Zerbi and Marseille have agreed to the departure of the Italian head coach by mutual consent after a series of damaging defeats, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Como stun Napoli, make 1st Copa semi in 40 yrs
items.summary: Keeper Jean Butez was Como's hero as they beat six-times winners Napoli in a penalty shootout to reach their first Coppa Italia semifinal in 40 years.

items.title: Hearts blaast hoop door Tynecastle met late zege in Edinburgh Derby
items.summary: Het Tynecastle Stadium van Heart of Midlothian schudde dinsdag op zijn grondvesten. Door een laat doelpunt van Tomas Magnusson trok Hearts niet alleen de Edinburgh Derby naar zich toe, maar nam de Schotse koploper ook afstand van Rangers FC en Celtic.

items.title: Scottish Premiership: Late winner for Hearts in tense Edinburgh derby
items.summary: Tomas Magnusson scores in 88th minute to seal win Jambos now have a six-point lead at the top of the table Tomas Magnusson scored a late winner as the Scottish Premiership leaders Hearts defeated Hibernian 1-0 in a tense Edinburgh derby to tighten their grip on top spot. Magnusson, on as a substitute, popped up with the game’s decisive moment in the 88th minute to spark jubilant scenes inside Tynecastle. Continue reading...

items.title: 'De Zerbi op weg naar de uitgang bij Marseille'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Man Utd's winning run ends with draw at West Ham
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko rescues a point for Manchester United as thier winning run under interim head coach Michael Carrick ends in a 1-1 draw at West Ham.

items.title: Leeds come back from two down to earn draw at Chelsea
items.summary: Chelsea miss the chance to move into the top four of the Premier League after throwing away a two-goal lead against Leeds United at Stamford Bridge.

items.title: Waanzinnige comeback in Championship, nieuwe stap Altena richting titel
items.summary: Met vier wedstrijden in de Premier League en een kraker in Coppa Italia beloofde het een volle voetbalavond te worden. Lees de volledige avond terug in onderstaande VI Live.

items.title: Bournemouth fight back to beat 10-man Everton
items.summary: Brazilian teenager Rayan continues his blistering start to life in the Premier League as Bournemouth come from behind to defeat 10-man Everton at Hill Dickinson Stadium.

items.title: Hertha sneuvelt na penalty's: Bundesliga-feestje in halve finale DFB-Pokal
items.summary: Hertha BSC heeft dinsdagavond op het nippertje naast de eerste halvefinaleplaats in de DFB-Pokal sinds 2016 gegrepen. SC Freiburg won na penalty's van de Tweede Bundesliga-club, waardoor er louter clubs uit de Bundesliga bij de laatste vier staan.

items.title: Premier League recap: West Ham deny Man United fifth straight win
items.summary: West Ham United ended Manchester United's winning streak, while Chelsea blew a 2-0 lead to draw against Leeds United in another eventful day of Premier League action.

items.title: 'No win, no trim' - has a haircut become a problem for Man Utd?
items.summary: A story about a haircut that has gone viral - meet Frank Ilett, the Manchester United fan with the infamous bouffant.

items.title: Boos after Newcastle hand Tottenham another home defeat
items.summary: Tottenham fans are left frustrated as Newcastle inflict a seventh home defeat of the season on Thomas Frank's side.

items.title: Same old Chelsea or 'clear change' in Rosenior's first month?
items.summary: Paul Merson believes Chelsea are still the same side as they were under Enzo Maresca, but head coach Liam Rosenior insists there has been a "clear change".

items.title: Sesko rescues late point for Manchester United to deny West Ham precious win
items.summary: Stop all the clippers, don’t cut off the barnet: Manchester United’s winning run is over. Frank Ilett, the man who pledged never to visit a barber again until United won five matches in a row, has been denied a viral haircut, but their resilience came to the fore after the West Ham were denied what would have been a deserved victory deep into added time. A studded finish from fan favourite Tomas Soucek was cancelled out by a strike from Benjamin Sesko to leave this match honours even. United lacked energy and snap, West Ham thought they had the game in the bag as the clock ticked past 90 minutes. The Hammers remain in 18th place, five points behind Spurs in 16th. Continue reading...

items.title: Man Utd-fan Frank Ilett kan na 493 dagen nog steeds niet naar de kapper
items.summary: Frank Ilett hoopte na 493 dagen eindelijk weer een afspraak te kunnen maken bij de kapper, maar de supporter van Manchester United kwam bedrogen uit. Door het puntenverlies van The Red Devils bij West Ham United is de veelbesproken social media-actie van Ilett voorlopig nog niet voorbij.

items.title: Okafor grabs comeback draw for Leeds as wasteful Chelsea’s bad habits return
items.summary: Chelsea’s habit of squandering leads did not leave with Enzo Maresca. The lapses in concentration have piled up and there was another to add to the list after Stamford Bridge witnessed an inexplicable collapse against Leeds, who took another step towards staying up fighting back from 2-0 down to keep themselves six points above the bottom three. The turnaround was not on the cards when Cole Palmer, who had earlier collected his first assist in the Premier League this season, extended Chelsea’s advantage by scoring from the spot for the third time in two games. By the end, though, Liam Rosenior was ­watching the prospect of an eighth win in 10 games slip away. The implosion was staggering. Lukas Nmecha and Noah Okafor had capitalised on dreadful defending to make it 2-2 and, while Chelsea rallied, their hopes of a late winner ended when Palmer produced a contender for miss of the season. Continue reading...

items.title: Rayan and Adli stage Bournemouth fightback against 10-man Everton
items.summary: Hill Dickinson Stadium is no place like home for Everton. Not yet, anyway. Sixth place and momentum in the push for European qualification was the tantalising prize for beating Bournemouth but, not for the first time at their plush new stadium, David Moyes’s team let it slip through their grasp. Andoni Iraola’s visitors extended their unbeaten Premier League run to six games courtesy of second-half headers from Rayan, the 19-year-old Brazilian who could well be their latest gem, and Amine Adli. Bournemouth’s goals and a red card for the Everton defender Jake O’Brien arrived in an eight-minute spell in which the hosts imploded to leave themselves without a home win since 6 December. Moyes’s side have collected 17 points from their last eight away games but only eight from the last nine at home. Their quest for Europe is floundering on home soil. Continue reading...
